methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Gravitics is a Seed-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $20M in equity plus $60M in STRATFI financing. The broader Space & Aerospace sector includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, though direct Seed-stage peers within this universe remain sparse.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using the $20M total equity raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next stage yields an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-median analysis is not actionable due to insufficient narrow-stage peer data—while 23 same-sector companies exist, fewer than three meet tighter filters for stage and valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring is similarly inapplicable, as Gravitics does not disclose revenue; the sector median P/S of 45.3x across public Space & Aerospace names cannot be reliably applied without a revenue figure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, centered on $50M. This range reflects the bounds of stage-progression logic applied to available fundraising data. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable Seed-stage private transactions in the space sector, opaque revenue trajectory, and the treatment of the $60M STRATFI non-equity instrument in determining true equity valuation. Additional revenue or near-term milestone data would strengthen conviction in any point within this range.
